name: Emilia aragon
ai_summary: Emilia has three young children and pregnancy experience, making her familiar with infant and toddler care. She is willing to drive 30-40 minutes for a full-time role, values attentiveness, and wants to support children's learning and development.

transcripts.q3:
So I have three children ranging from 3 months old 25, going to be 60 this year and then my daughter is two years old. She'll be three years old this year and we're focusing on trying to get her potty trained because of my pregnancy with my three month old. That's why I haven't been working because I just been having a hard time with being able to work with being pregnant. My last job I was about 16 or so.
 So many weeks pregnant and they were giving me a really hard time about that pregnancy and wanting to write me up about having to make appointments and not feeling good. So, yeah, I have three kids. That's kind of where my familiarity is with taking care of kids.

 So yeah.

 So yeah, please let me know if and I've know lots of other ones may open up earlier but as long as it's within that timeframe then I'll be good time.
transcripts.q6:
So, I've never dealt with any like kids that weren't conflicts and like an actual like classroom setting, but I've had to deal with my kids, fighting my 23-year old, and my five-year-old because they fight a lot. Even though there's that age difference, they do fight and I have to tell them like you know, you have to share if you don't share them. No one gets to play with this or
 If they're wanting something that I tell them that, well, they had it first that you have to ask. Maybe if you asked they'll let you play with it and then if the other one is being stubborn and regards to sharing, I'll tell them you know, you how you can play with it a little longer, but then you need to give it over to your sibling because it's not just yours. You can't just being mean. That's not okay. We need to be nice to one another and share these.
 Items and if there's a conflict where they're trying to hurt one another then I'll separate them, make them sit down, no privileges or anything until they calm down they apologized and they hug it out. But it with other kids, they don't have to hug but they need to say that they're sorry and I find some kind of alternate thing to do.

 And I would be very attentive to what is asked of me and just making sure that they're all right, good and making sure that they're learning, not just playing and that they have everything they need and yeah.
